name: clearance_register_leads
method: PARIVESH EC/FC registers (2025-26 filings, live statuses, manufacturing activities, corporate proponents only, deduped against layer 16's 321 leads) -> public-news sweep -> visibility verdict -> CREDIT-RATING enrichment (CARE Ratings first, CRISIL/ICRA/India Ratings where CARE does not rate). A clearance filing is stronger evidence of intent than an announcement: fees paid, EIA filed, site on record. Rating rationales are the third channel: for unlisted/quiet companies they are often the ONLY document quantifying the capex, site and funding mix behind the filings.
sources: CARE Ratings rationales (careratings.com) -- CRISIL/ICRA/India Ratings used where CARE does not rate the entity, flagged as such
why_quiet_matters: QUIET = verified investment intent with NO public announcement -- the same below-radar category the PIB-visibility work found most valuable for investment-promotion targeting.

leads.news_summary: Oct 2025: Rs 10,255 cr to add 22.8 MTPA (to 240.76 MTPA); Q3 FY26 commissioned 1.2 MTPA at Nathdwara, Rajasthan; quarterly capex ~Rs 2,357 cr.
leads.match_with_filings: Strong -- Rajasthan commissioning + multi-state program aligns with 23 multi-state cement EC filings.

leads.news_summary: Entity publicly known only for Rs 1,600 cr 6 MTPA Nawada, Bihar grinding unit; parent Ambuja's broader capex public, but NO Maharashtra project under this entity in news.
leads.match_with_filings: STATE MISMATCH -- filings are Maharashtra cement; all news is Bihar/parent-level. The Maharashtra pipeline is not publicly visible.

credit_rating.rating: none found -- entity itself unrated (100% subsidiary of Ambuja Cements Ltd, which is CARE AAA/A1+ per CARE PR of Oct-2025 and CRISIL AAA/Stable/A1+)
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: none found for the subsidiary -- incorporated 14-Sep-2023 in Gujarat as Adani/Ambuja's RMC arm; parent-level rationales discuss Ambuja Cements' cement capex but give no capex breakout for Ambuja Concrete North or its Maharashtra ready-mix plants
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: nothing new on the 13 Maharashtra EC filings -- the subsidiary borrows no debt in its own name so no rationale exists; only parent disclosures cover it, and they don't itemize the RMC rollout

leads.news_summary: Rs 3,000 cr expansion announced across Chhattisgarh/UP/Bihar/Jharkhand; ~$203M Chhattisgarh MoU Nov 2025; 30 MTPA by FY30 target -- but no West Bengal plant in any news.
leads.match_with_filings: Partial -- eastern push is public but the WEST BENGAL location of its 11 EC filings (latest 2026-07) is absent from news; WB looks like the unannounced next leg.

credit_rating.rating: CARE AA; Stable (LT, Rs 2,770.47 cr) / CARE A1+ (ST, Rs 1,300 cr), reaffirmed
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: ~Rs 3,000 cr capex over next three years (~Rs 2,000 cr debt + internal accruals) to go from 16.5 to 22.45 MTPA between FY26-FY28, targeting 30 MTPA by FY30. Components: Surat split grinding unit +1.35 MTPA in H1FY26; Durg expansion +4.6 MTPA (2.3 MTPA clinker line + FOUR grinding units of 4.6 MTPA total) at Rs 2,500 cr by Mar-2028; ACPL (Agrani Cement, 85% stake for Rs 325.11 cr) clinkerisation 1.0 MTPA + grinding 1.5 MTPA 'currently awaiting necessary clearances', with Assam limestone reserves ~335 MT via Trivikram Consortium; net debt/PBILDT to stay ~2.0-2.5x
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: The rationale's four unnamed-location grinding units under the Durg expansion plus the ACPL project explicitly 'awaiting clearances' are the likely counterpart of the 11 West Bengal EC filings -- giving a Rs 2,500 cr project cost and Mar-2028 deadline news never tied to WB

leads.news_summary: Apr 2025: ~Rs 1.5 trillion split ~Rs 75,000 cr each into new energy and petrochemical expansion centered on Jamnagar, Gujarat; 2,000 acres at Kandla for green hydrogen.
leads.match_with_filings: Strong -- Gujarat petrochemical-complex EC filings align with announced Jamnagar petrochemical expansion.

leads.news_summary: SeQuent-Viyash merger (2025), $1B revenue target by 2032, stated intent to increase capacities -- but no specific plant, project or capex announced.
leads.match_with_filings: Weak -- general growth intent public; the specific Maharashtra/Telangana API expansions in its 10 EC filings are entirely absent from news.

credit_rating.rating: ICRA: AA- (Stable), assigned (Rs 185 cr fund-based working capital facilities)
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: Explicitly states the company 'is not expected to incur any significant expansion capex over the next 3-4 years and will undertake capex in the range of Rs. 100-150 crore per annum'; FY2027 planned capex ~Rs 100-150 cr; cash and liquid investments Rs 276.1 cr (Dec-2025); Total Debt/OPBDITA improved to 0.9x post the Nov-2025 merger of Viyash Lifesciences into Sequent Scientific (renamed Viyash Scientific); Carlyle Group majority shareholder; 9M FY26 operating margin 18.9%
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: Reveals a direct contradiction worth flagging: management told ICRA there is no significant expansion capex for 3-4 years, yet the company has 10 live EC filings -- either the filings are within the modest Rs 100-150 cr/yr budget or plans post-date the April-2026 rating

leads.news_summary: 4 Apr 2026 board approved new integrated plant at Daistong, East Jaintia Hills, MEGHALAYA -- 0.95 MTPA clinker / 0.99 MTPA cement, ~Rs 1,800 cr, complete Q4 FY28.
leads.match_with_filings: EXACT match -- Meghalaya filings latest 2026-04 line up precisely with the April 2026 board approval.

leads.news_summary: Jun 2026: Odisha approved Rs 7,837 cr to triple integrated steel capacity to ~2.85 MnT; separate Dhenkanal expansion 3.55->7.53 MTPA pegged Rs 11,001 cr; Danieli mills ordered.
leads.match_with_filings: Strong -- Odisha metallurgy/beneficiation filings correspond to Kamanda + Dhenkanal expansions.

leads.news_summary: ~$580M IPO filing (Rs 1,500 cr fresh) and world's largest organic titanate plant at Mundra public -- but no announced capex or new Gujarat expansion for 2025-26.
leads.match_with_filings: Partial -- Gujarat footprint public but the specific expansions behind 6 EC filings (latest 2026-02) not covered.

credit_rating.rating: CRISIL: AA/Stable / A1+ (reaffirmed; bank facilities enhanced to Rs 1,741.6 cr)
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: No specific expansion projects disclosed; maintenance capex estimated at Rs 150-200 crore annually from FY2026; Impact Fluid Solutions (US) acquisition was fully debt-funded, pushing net debt to Rs 2,995 cr (Mar-2025); SEBI approved DRHP June 2025 and ~Rs 1,500 cr of IPO proceeds earmarked for debt retirement and general corporate purposes; operations at Mundra and Dahej (Gujarat), Maharashtra, Brazil, USA
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: Adds the Rs 2,995 cr net debt figure, the Rs 1,500 cr IPO-proceeds-to-debt plan, and confirms no big organic capex is budgeted -- so the 6 Gujarat EC filings likely represent post-IPO growth projects not yet in the rating case

leads.news_summary: Board cleared Rs 4,805 cr 7 MTPA expansion: 4 MTPA clinker + 3 MTPA grinding at Jaisalmer, Rajasthan + two 2 MTPA split grinding units (Rajasthan, Punjab).
leads.match_with_filings: Strong -- the Punjab grinding unit corresponds directly to the Punjab cement filings (latest 2026-04).

leads.news_summary: Completed Rs 27,000 cr Phase II Kalinganagar (Odisha) May 2025 (3->8 MTPA); stated scope to double Kalinganagar to 16 MTPA; Rs 15,000 cr FY26 capex, Rs 11,000 cr for India.
leads.match_with_filings: Strong -- Odisha metallurgy filings (latest 2026-07) align with next-phase Kalinganagar growth.

leads.news_summary: No mainstream press announcement found. Only regulatory-derived trackers (Global Energy Monitor wiki, WBPCB filing PDFs) document the Kharagpur (West Medinipur, WB) expansion to ~1.8-2.0 MTPA integrated steel plant; GEM notes expansion underway as of Jan 2025 with no completion updates as of Oct 2025. No capex figure in any news outlet.
leads.match_with_filings: The documented Kharagpur/Shyamraipur West Bengal steel expansion matches the metallurgy EC filings, but visibility comes only from clearance-document trackers, not news media.

credit_rating.rating: CRISIL: AA/Stable (long-term, upgraded from AA-/Positive) / A1+ (short-term)
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: Rashmi Group consolidated capex of ~Rs 8,000-10,000 crore over FY2026-28, funded primarily through internal accrual (annual cash accrual expected over Rs 5,000 cr/fiscal, limited external debt). Manufacturing at Kharagpur and Jamuria, West Bengal; group capacities >32 million tonnes as of Jan-2025; Rashmi Green Hydrogen Steel's 600,000 MTPA ductile iron pipe unit to start April 2025; consolidated revenue Rs 28,550 cr FY24, ~Rs 33,000 cr expected FY25
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: Quantifies the group-level war chest behind the Kharagpur ~2 MTPA expansion -- Rs 8,000-10,000 cr FY26-28 funded largely by internal accruals -- which no clearance tracker or news source discloses

leads.news_summary: Company expansion news exists but for Andhra Pradesh, not Telangana: inaugurated a new HPAPI oncology facility (Unit-8) in Vizag in March 2026 and has raised ~$175M total funding. Revenue Rs 4,420 cr FY25. No news of the Telangana (Sangareddy/Pashamylaram) API expansion in the EC filings.
leads.match_with_filings: News covers Vizag CDMO/oncology expansion, not the Telangana synthetic-organic-chemicals/API projects in the clearance filings -- state does not match.

credit_rating.rating: CRISIL: AA-/Stable (NCDs aggregating Rs 2,500 cr, reaffirmed)
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: Moderate annual capex plan of ~Rs 200 crore with 'no major capex plan' anticipated; acquisition (Viatris API business) funding mix disclosed: Rs 1,965 cr NCDs in four tranches + Rs 830 cr CCDs + Rs 955 cr equity + Rs 230-240 cr additional equity for working capital. Separately, ICRA (Nov-2025) notes Rs 86 cr invested in CCDs Aug-Oct 2025 with Rs 50 cr more by Jan-2026 toward acquiring a stake in Hydra Active Pharma Sciences. No plant-level Telangana/Vizag project detail in the rationale
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: Adds the ~Rs 200 cr/yr capex budget, the full Rs 2,500 cr acquisition debt structure, and an unreported Rs 136 cr Hydra Active Pharma stake purchase -- the Telangana API EC filings sit inside that Rs 200 cr/yr envelope, not a separately announced project

leads.news_summary: Rs 650 crore IPO (Rs 600 cr fresh + Rs 50 cr OFS) opened April 2025 explicitly to fund expansion of manufacturing facilities (five of six plants in Karnataka: Gauribidanur, Bellary, Koppal, Chikkantapur) and debt reduction; includes a 10 MW WHRB power plant and stainless steel expansion on a 14.97 lakh MTPA base.
leads.match_with_filings: IPO-funded Karnataka steel capacity expansion matches the Karnataka metallurgy EC filings in state and activity.

leads.news_summary: Heavily publicized ~Rs 60,000 crore ($7.7B programme incl. $5.1B upstream) Hazira, Gujarat expansion from 9 to 15 MTPA targeted by 2026, with auto-steel CGL/CGAL lines operational 2025 and long-term roadmap to 25-26 MTPA by 2030.
leads.match_with_filings: Directly matches the Gujarat metallurgy + mineral beneficiation EC filings (Hazira integrated steel plant expansion, incl. a June 2026 EC-tracked Hazira expansion item).

leads.news_summary: Rs 4,001 crore specialty chemicals manufacturing project at Tata Steel's Gopalpur Industrial Park, Odisha; foundation stone laid by Odisha CM in June 2025 as part of Rs 20,500 cr of projects. Superform (formerly UPL Specialty Chemicals) positioned in Odisha's Rs 2 lakh cr chemical investment push.
leads.match_with_filings: Gopalpur, Odisha specialty chemicals project matches the Odisha chemicals+pesticides EC filings in state and activity.

leads.news_summary: Board approved fresh capex of over Rs 33,000 crore for Vijayanagar (Karnataka) Phase-2 expansion from 15 to 26 MTPA (commissioning by ~FY30); company-wide FY26 capex plan of Rs 20,000 crore flags Vijayanagar as a flagship project alongside Dolvi Phase-III.
leads.match_with_filings: Vijayanagar Karnataka steel expansion directly matches the Karnataka metallurgy EC filings.

leads.news_summary: Rs 45,000 crore (~$5.2B) expansion announced March 2025 across aluminium/copper/specialty alumina within a $10B five-year plan; Gujarat-specific pieces include a 300,000-tonne Dahej copper smelter expansion (to be world's largest outside China) and a ~Rs 2,400 crore e-waste/copper recycling plant at Dahej.
leads.match_with_filings: State matches (Gujarat/Dahej) and metallurgy matches, though Hindalco's publicized Gujarat projects are copper-focused while the filings are tagged aluminium/metallurgy -- likely the same Dahej complex.

leads.news_summary: February 2026 exchange filing and press coverage of a Rs 1,403 crore capacity expansion across AP and TN units -- Ariyalur (TN) plant capacity raised to 5.5 MTPA, Rs 1,250 cr for Kalavatala (AP), Rs 153 cr for a 10 MW WHRS at Ramasamy Raja Nagar (TN); part of a growth strategy targeting Rs 16,000 cr revenue.
leads.match_with_filings: Tamil Nadu cement capacity and WHRS expansions match the TN cement + mineral beneficiation EC filings.

leads.news_summary: News exists on the Nissan Bharat Rasayan JV building a new Ryzonic rice-herbicide facility in India (operations from FY2028) and a ~Rs 220 crore phase-1 capex for a 25,000-ton plant, but no press specifically announces Bharat Rasayan Ltd's own Gujarat pesticide expansions behind the 2026 EC filings.
leads.match_with_filings: Activity (pesticides/agrochemicals) and likely geography (Dahej, Gujarat) are consistent, but the news is about the JV entity rather than the parent's own EC-filed projects.

credit_rating.rating: CARE AA-; Stable (LT) / CARE A1+ (ST and CP), reaffirmed; approach revised standalone-to-consolidated with Nissan Bharat Rasayan JV
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: States BRL 'does not have major capex plans except routine capex and refurbishment for Rs 60-70 crore annually'; restatement of Block D of Dahej plant completed Feb-2024 and at full capacity; units at Mokhra, Haryana (5,000 MTPA) and Dahej, Gujarat (29,300 MTPA); Nissan Bharat Rasayan JV (30:70 with Nissan Chemical) income Rs 272.49 cr FY24, PAT margin 19%; zero long-term debt, gearing 0.08x, net worth Rs 987.8 cr, and 'sufficient gearing headroom to raise additional debt for its capex needs'
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: Shows the 5 Gujarat pesticide EC filings are NOT in the rating case (only Rs 60-70 cr/yr routine capex disclosed as of Dec-2024) -- signalling an undisclosed new expansion the debt-free balance sheet (0.08x gearing) can easily fund

leads.news_summary: Rs 800 crore approved for a new NPK granulation train at Kakinada (AP, +7.5 lakh tons to 30 lakh TPA) plus a fungicide multi-product plant at Ankleshwar (Gujarat); separately ~Rs 1,000 crore for 650 TPD phosphoric acid and 2,000 TPD sulphuric acid plants, and AP govt granted a 45% subsidy on a Rs 1,539 crore Kakinada investment (GO dated Feb 2025).
leads.match_with_filings: Multi-state fertilizer/chemicals expansions (AP fertilizer, Gujarat crop-protection) match the multi-state fertilizer/chemicals EC filings.

leads.news_summary: Identity: NOT a spin-off -- Ami Organics RENAMED to Acutaas Chemicals May 2025 (same NSE entity), pivoting to battery/semiconductor chemicals. Rs 1,000+ cr capex FY23-FY30; ~Rs 220 cr battery-chemicals plant at Jhagadia (Gujarat) Ph-1 inaugurated Jan 2026; ~Rs 200 cr Korea semiconductor JV.
leads.match_with_filings: The 4 specialty-chem filings align with the Gujarat battery-chemicals buildout at Jhagadia.

leads.news_summary: Heavy public capex (~Rs 3,500 cr + subsidiary phenol/IPA plants) but Gujarat/Dahej-centric. The TELANGANA expansion (Jeedimetla Unit-I, ~Rs 20 cr) surfaces only in the EC document itself.
leads.match_with_filings: The 4 Telangana filings = a small Hyderabad unit expansion essentially invisible in national news.

credit_rating.rating: ICRA: AA (Stable) / A1+, reaffirmed with outlook revised to Stable from Positive
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: Group capex of Rs 11,000-11,500 crore over FY2026-FY2028: 165 KTPA polycarbonate resin (plant bought from Trinseo for $52.5M, relocating from Stade, Germany), 300 KTPA phenol, 185 KTPA acetone, 100 KTPA IPA, plus BPA backward integration in advanced finalisation; separate Rs 2,400 cr modular capex under Deepak Chem Tech (up from Rs 2,016 cr) commissioning through FY2026; debt-funded, cash generation from FY2029; manufacturing sites listed as Nandesari and Dahej (Gujarat), Taloja and Roha (Maharashtra) and Hyderabad (Telangana)
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: Confirms Hyderabad/Telangana is an existing DNL site but the Jeedimetla EC-filed expansions are NOT itemized in the Rs 11,000+ cr program -- they sit outside the headline capex narrative, consistent with their national invisibility

leads.news_summary: Jul 2025: Rs 750+ cr chemicals expansion -- doubling fatty alcohol (+35,000 TPA), erucic acid, glycerine, tripling fermentation -- targeting $1B chemicals business by 2030.
leads.match_with_filings: The 4 chemicals filings (latest Dec 2025) map directly onto the announced multi-product expansion.

leads.news_summary: NO investment/expansion news at all. 1999-incorporated Mumbai company, ~48 employees, plant at Boisar, Maharashtra; specialty chemicals for textiles/coatings/leather; FY25 net sales +21%, assets +37% -- quiet growth.
leads.match_with_filings: The 4 polymer EC filings (latest Mar 2026) have ZERO public counterpart -- a genuinely below-radar expansion, likely Boisar.

credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: none found -- no rating rationale from any agency; company registries show a 1999-incorporated Mumbai company with a Tarapur MIDC (Boisar) unit and FY25 revenue of ~Rs 56.7 cr
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: nothing new from ratings, but the FY25 revenue figure (~Rs 57 cr) from registry data implies the 4 EC filings represent a very large expansion relative to current scale

leads.news_summary: No news whatsoever. LLP incorporated 24 Jan 2025 at Rajkot (Gondal), Gujarat, Rs 1 lakh capital, 10 partners. A brand-new entity filing 4 ECs within ~15 months = a greenfield plant built entirely off the news radar.
leads.match_with_filings: The 4 specialty-chem filings (latest Apr 2026) are the ONLY public evidence this newly-formed Gujarat LLP is building anything.

credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: none found -- no CARE/CRISIL/ICRA/India Ratings rationale exists; only MCA registration profiles (LLPIN ACL-5717, incorporated 24-Jan-2025, Rajkot) surface in searches
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: nothing new -- the LLP is unrated and has zero financial disclosure anywhere; the 4 EC filings remain the only public evidence of its plans

leads.news_summary: Retail push well covered (2,000-2,500 stations, ~4% petrol/6%+ diesel private share, 5,000+ EV charge points, 30,000 chargers by 2030) but NO coverage of the specific Haryana/multi-state land acquisitions; no rupee figure for the rollout.
leads.match_with_filings: The 58 forest-clearance land diversions (latest Jul 2026, Haryana-heavy) quantify a rollout whose granular geography and pace are invisible in public announcements.

credit_rating.rating: CARE AAA; Stable (LT bank facilities Rs 1,055 cr) / with LT/ST facilities also rated
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: No total capex figure, but funding trail disclosed: term debt of Rs 500 cr availed FY23 plus Rs 400 cr in H1FY24 to fund capex during adverse conditions; 7MFY25 capex funded entirely from internal accruals; network grown from ~1,400 outlets (at transfer from RIL) to ~1,850 as of 31-Oct-2024, with plans to expand fuel retail, EV fixed charging + battery swapping across Metro/Tier-I/Tier-II in phases, CNG/Bio-CNG stations, convenience stores and forecourt QSRs
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: Adds the only public capex financing data for the 58 forest-clearance fuel-station rollout -- Rs 900 cr of historical capex term debt, now self-funded from accruals, and the 1,850-outlet count as of Oct-2024

leads.news_summary: Gujarat project flow heavily publicized (92.15 MWp GUVNL hybrid commissioned early, Rs 272 cr NaBFID financing, 10 GW by 2030) but news is at project/PPA level -- the 20 forest-land diversions are not reported as such.
leads.match_with_filings: The 20 Gujarat FC filings are consistent with the announced hybrid pipeline but no announcement names the land parcels.

credit_rating.rating: ICRA: A (Positive) / A2+ (bank lines); Provisional [ICRA]AA+(CE) (Stable) on Rs 700 cr NCDs (GuarantCo-backed)
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: Capex of ~Rs 4,100 crore for ~0.9 GW under-development utility-scale IPP projects, ~75% debt-funded; cost estimate raised by ~Rs 700 cr due to DC upsizing; projects: 250-MW (275 MWdc) GUVNL solar at Rs 2.65/unit and 370-MW (658 MW installed) GUVNL hybrid at Rs 3.24/unit; 200-MWac Khavda solar + 50-MW Bharuch hybrid installed Mar-2025 (commissioning pending); Rs 1,000 cr QIP Aug-2024 + Rs 500 cr equity at Sun Drops fund the equity portion; entire operational capacity in Gujarat (Bharuch, Bhavnagar, Khavda)
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: Dwarfs the known Rs 272 cr NaBFID facility -- the rationale sizes the full Gujarat pipeline behind the 20 forest clearances at ~Rs 4,100 cr, 75% debt, with tariffs, counterparty and RoW/land availability flagged as the key execution risk

leads.news_summary: National retail expansion public -- crossed 7,000 pumps (Jun 2026), 500+ outlets in 18 months (~1/day), India's largest private fuel retailer, ~400 more planned -- but nothing Punjab-specific and no capex figure.
leads.match_with_filings: The 20 Punjab FC filings are the state-level footprint of a nationally-announced but geographically undisclosed rollout.

credit_rating.rating: CARE AA- (Rating Watch with Negative Implications) on LT facilities Rs 11,453.71 cr and NCDs Rs 256.84 cr / CARE A1+ (RWN) on ST Rs 14,350 cr -- placed on watch after US sanctions on Rosneft/Lukoil effective 21-Nov-2025
credit_rating.rationale_capex_disclosure: 6,700+ operational retail outlets as of 30-Jun-2025, run primarily on dealer-owned-dealer-operated (DODO) model 'resulting in minimal capital requirement for its retail outlet expansion plan'; plans additional rail-fed fuel depots (existing at Wardha, Maharashtra and Pali, Rajasthan) to cut dependence on PSU OMCs; 450 KTPA polypropylene plant commissioned July-2024 at ~75% utilisation; routine capex partly debt-funded; net-debt gearing 0.12x (Jun-2025); any large debt-funded expansion is a stated rating monitorable
credit_rating.adds_beyond_news: Explains why no capex figure exists for the 7,000-pump network: the DODO model makes Nayara's own outlay minimal -- plus adds the sanctions-driven RWN status and the unreported depot-buildout plan behind the Punjab forest clearances

leads.news_summary: L.N. Mittal at Progressive Punjab Investors Summit (Dec 2025): Rs 2,600 cr at Guru Gobind Singh Refinery, Bathinda -- polypropylene downstream + fine/specialty chemicals -- plus fuel-retail entry with 500 outlets nationally.
leads.match_with_filings: The 14 Punjab+UP FC filings match both the Bathinda expansion and land for the 500-outlet retail rollout.

candidate_pool.note: shortlist is the TOP of a 1,445-company pool of live corporate industrial filers -- the pool file (scratchpad ec_lead_candidates.json) supports deeper batches
